article_title: Scientists Discover 24 New Deep-Sea Species and an Entirely New Branch of Life in the Pacific's Clarion-Clipperton Zone

content_preview.title: Scientists Discover 24 New Deep-Sea Species and an Entirely New Branch of Life in the Pacific's Clarion-Clipperton Zone
content_preview.summary: Researchers identify 24 amphipod species, a new family, and a new superfamily in a region targeted for deep-sea mining.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

An international team of marine biologists has identified 24 previously unknown species of amphipod crustaceans in the Clarion-Clipperton Zone (CCZ), a vast stretch of the central Pacific Ocean floor between Hawaii and Mexico. Among the discoveries is an entirely new superfamily, Mirabestioidea, representing a rare addition to the evolutionary tree of life. The findings, published in a special open-access issue of the journal *ZooKeys*, arrive as the region faces mounting commercial...
